name: Proto-Maghrebi Arabic

ancestors.notes: Descent from Classical Arabic via 7thвҖ“8th c. expansion

notes: Proto-Maghrebi Arabic вҖ” abstract ancestor node for Moroccan, Algerian, Tunisian, Libyan, and Hassaniya Arabic. Key shared Maghrebi features: vowel reduction/deletion (short vowels вҶ’ schwa [Йҷ] or Гҳ, yielding heavy consonant clusters вҖ” the defining Maghrebi trait); Ш¬ вҶ’ /К’/ (voiced postalveolar fricative, sedentary Maghreb) not /dК’/; Ш« вҶ’ /t/; Ш° вҶ’ /d/; ЩӮ вҶ’ /q/ preserved in sedentary/pre-Hilali varieties (/ЙЎ/ in Bedouin/rural); Berber (Amazigh) substrate reinforcing pharyngeals and emphatics and licensing the cluster phonotactics. ORTHOGRAPHIC READING BASE: graphemes/positional_graphemes inherit from the modern MSA leaf `ar` (not Classical `arb`), because the modern spoken varieties are written in MSA orthography вҖ” so they must read it the way `ar` does: the onset-glide digraphs ЩҠЩҺ/ЩҲЩҺ (/ja/,/wa/, not diphthongs), the hamza carriers ШЈ/ШҘ as bare /К”/ before an explicit harakah, word-final ЩҠ/ЩҲ preferring the long-vowel reading, and pausal tДҒКҫ marbЕ«б№ӯa. Genealogy is unchanged (parent/ancestors still point at Classical); only the data-inheritance base moves. Each variety's own phonological overrides (interdentals, qДҒf reflex, affrication) sit in its own tables and continue to win over the inherited layer.

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E.
sources.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic
sources.notes: Read (full open-access PDF; printed pages). p.16: Maghribi jД«m has no initial occlusive element, realized /Еҫ/ [К’]. p.15: sedentary interdentalвҶ’dental-stop merger. p.17: *q maintained (sedentary) vs /ЙЎ/ (Bedouin). p.13: unstressed-vowel deletion вҶ’ initial clusters.

sources.author: Holes, C.
sources.title: Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties

location.notes: Glottolog's computed centroid for the North African Arabic family вҖ” a summary of a whole clade's range, not a place anyone speaks.

stress.notes: Arabic stress is QUANTITY-SENSITIVE: it falls on a syllable because that syllable is heavy, not because of any orthographic ending, so no ending table can express it. The cascade (Ryding, A Reference Grammar of MSA, CUP 2005, В§2.3; Watson, The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic, OUP 2002, ch.3): a superheavy final syllable (CVЛҗC/CVCC) takes the stress (kiЛҲtaЛҗb); otherwise a heavy penult (CVЛҗ/CVC) does (muЛҲdarris); otherwise the antepenult (ЛҲmadrasa). max_onset=1 because Arabic onsets are obligatory and hold exactly one consonant вҖ” a medial cluster splits, closing the preceding syllable, and that boundary is what makes the penult of mudarris heavy. INPUT CONTRACT: weight is only recoverable from a complete transcription, so this requires diacritized input; an undiacritized skeleton has no vowels to weigh. The registered wikipron gold carries no stress marks, but the benchmark strips stress from both sides by default, so this costs no PER.
